title: FQDN needed in containers
body:
### Issue Description

When a container tries to connect to a server somehostname, the connection fails unless the FQDN is used (somehostname.fully.qualified.name). However connecting to somehostname works on the host.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a container with podman-compose
2. In the container, try to connect to another server in the same network as the host using only the server's hostname


### Describe the results you received

host:
[vmatassa@lxkvmap178 medphys-server]$ curl WNETDCAD32
curl: (7) Failed to connect to WNETDCAD32 port 80: Connection refused
[vmatassa@lxkvmap178 medphys-server]$ curl WNETDCAD32.fully.qualified.name
curl: (7) Failed to connect to WNETDCAD32.fully.qualified.name port 80: Connection refused

container:
root@fb2be2e37aa7:/var/www/html# curl WNETDCAD32
curl: (6) Could not resolve host: WNETDCAD32
root@fb2be2e37aa7:/var/www/html# curl WNETDCAD32.fully.qualified.name
curl: (7) Failed to connect to WNETDCAD32.fully.qualified.name port 80: Connection refused

### Describe the results you expected

host:
[vmatassa@lxkvmap178 medphys-server]$ curl WNETDCAD32
curl: (7) Failed to connect to WNETDCAD32 port 80: Connection refused
[vmatassa@lxkvmap178 medphys-server]$ curl WNETDCAD32.fully.qualified.name
curl: (7) Failed to connect to WNETDCAD32.fully.qualified.name port 80: Connection refused

container:
root@fb2be2e37aa7:/var/www/html# curl WNETDCAD32
curl: (7) Failed to connect to WNETDCAD32 port 80: Connection refused
root@fb2be2e37aa7:/var/www/html# curl WNETDCAD32.fully.qualified.name
curl: (7) Failed to connect to WNETDCAD32.fully.qualified.name port 80: Connection refused

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

OS is CentOS 9 stream running in a VM
podman-compose version: 1.0.7

10.89.0.149     47d6f7fddd0c medphys-server_api_1

comments.body: And what exactly resolves `WNETDCAD32` on the host, /etc/hosts, dns, mdns, ...?

Networking isn't my specialty, so I'm not exactly sure how to answer your question but I think I figured out the issue. On the host's /etc/resolv.conf, we have:

```
domain fully.qualified.name
search fully.qualified.name
nameserver      172.25.192.16
nameserver      172.25.192.20
nameserver      172.25.32.8
nameserver      172.25.32.136
```

while on the container:
```
search dns.podman
nameserver 10.89.0.1
```

Adding `search fully.qualified.name` to the container's /etc/resolv.conf fixed the issue for that container. Is there a way to permanently add the search domain to all network containers created by podman?

> Adding search fully.qualified.name to the container's /etc/resolv.conf fixed the issue for that container. Is there a way to permanently add the search domain to all network containers created by podman?

Yes in containers.conf there is a `dns_searches` option
Adding the search domain to /etc/containers/containers.conf fixed the issue. Thank you!
